author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: 1 John II. 12–17.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I write unto you, little children, because your sins are forgiven through His name.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I write unto you, fathers, because ye have known Him that is from the beginning.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I write unto you, young men, because ye have overcome the wicked one.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I write unto you, children, because ye have known the Father.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I write unto you, fathers, because ye have known Him that is from the beginning.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I write unto you, young men, because ye are strong, and the word of God abideth in you, and ye have overcome the wicked one.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For all that is in the world, is the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, which is not of the Father, but is of the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever (even as God also abideth for ever).

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: All things that are read from the Holy Scriptures in order to our instruction and salvation, it behoves us to hear with earnest heed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Yet most of all must those things be commended to our memory, which are of most force against heretics; whose insidious designs cease not to circumvent all that are weaker and more negligent.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Remember that our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ both died for us, and rose again; died, to wit, for our offenses, rose again for our justification.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Even as ye have just heard concerning the two disciples whom He met with in the way, how “their eyes were holden that they should not know Him:” and He found them despairing of the redemption that was in Christ, and deeming that now He had suffered and was dead as a man, not accounting that as Son of God He ever liveth; and deeming too that He was so dead in the flesh as not to come to life again, but just as one of the prophets: as those of you who were attentive have just now heard their own words.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then “He opened to them the Scriptures, beginning at Moses,” and going through all the prophets, showing them that all He had suffered had been foretold, lest they should be more staggered if the Lord should rise again, and the more fail to believe Him, if these things had not been told before concerning Him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the firmness of faith is in this, that all things which came to pass in Christ were foretold.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The disciples, then, knew Him not, save “in the breaking of bread.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And truly he that eateth and drinketh not judgment to himself in the breaking of bread doth know Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Afterward also those eleven “thought they saw a spirit.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He gave Himself to be handled by them, who also gave Himself to be crucified; to be crucified by enemies, to be handled by friends: yet the Physician of all, both of the ungodliness of those, and of the unbelief of these.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For ye heard when the Acts of the Apostles were read, how many thousands of Christ’s slayers believed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If those believed afterwards who had killed, should not those believe who for a little while doubted?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And yet even in regard of them, (a thing which ye ought especially to observe, and to commit to your memory, because that which shall make us strong against insidious errors, God has been pleased to put in the Scriptures, against which no man dares to speak, who in any sort wishes to seem a Christian), when He had given Himself to be handled by them, that did not suffice Him, but He would also confirm by means of the Scriptures the heart of them that believe: for He looked forward to us who should be afterwards; seeing that in Him we have nothing that we can handle, but have that which we may read.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if those believed only because they held and handled, what shall we do?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now, Christ is ascended into heaven; He is not to come save at the end, to judge the quick and the dead.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whereby shall we believe, but by that whereby it was His will that even those who handled Him should be confirmed?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For He opened to them the Scriptures and showed them that it behoved Christ to suffer, and that all things should be fulfilled which were written of Him in the Law of Moses, and the Prophets, and the Psalms.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He embraced in His discourse the whole ancient text of the Scriptures.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: All that there is of those former Scriptures tells of Christ; but only if it find ears.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He also “opened their understanding that they might understand the Scriptures.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence we also must pray for this, that He would open our understanding.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But what did the Lord show written of Him in the Law of Moses, and the Prophets, and the Psalms?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What did He show?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let Himself say.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The evangelist has put this briefly, that we might know what in all that great compass of the Scriptures we ought to believe and to understand.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Certainly there are many pages, and many books; the contents of them all is this which the Lord briefly spake to His disciples.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That “it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise again the third day.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou hast it now concerning the Bridegroom, that “it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise again:” the Bridegroom has been set forth to us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Concerning the Bride, let us see what He saith; that thou, when thou knowest the Bridegroom and the Bride, mayest not without reason come to the marriage.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For every celebration is a celebration of marriage: the Church’s nuptials are celebrated.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The King’s Son is about to marry a wife, and that King’s Son is Himself a King: and the guests frequenting the marriage are themselves the Bride.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Not, as in a carnal marriage, some are guests, and another is she that is married; in the Church they that come as guests, if they come to good purpose, become the Bride.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For all the Church is Christ’s Bride, of which the beginning and first fruits is the flesh of Christ: there was the Bride joined to the Bridegroom in the flesh.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: With good reason when He would betoken that same flesh, He brake bread, and with good reason “in the breaking of bread,” the eyes “of the disciples were opened, and they knew Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Well then, what did the Lord say was written of Him in the Law and Prophets and Psalms?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That “it behoved Christ to suffer.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Had He not added, “and to rise again,” well might those mourn whose eyes were holden; but “to rise again” is also foretold.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And wherefore this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why did it behove Christ to suffer and to rise again?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because of that Psalm which we especially commended to your attention on the fourth day, the first station, of last week.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why did it behove Christ to suffer and to rise again?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For this reason: “All the ends of the earth shall be reminded and converted unto the Lord, and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For that ye may know that it behoved Christ to suffer and to rise again; in this place also what hath He added, that after setting forth the Bridegroom He might also set forth the Bride?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And that there be preached,” saith He, “in His name, repentance and remission of sins throughout all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye have heard, brethren; hold it fast.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let no man doubt concerning the Church, that it is “throughout all nations:” let no man doubt that it began at Jerusalem, and hath filled all nations.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: We know the field where the Vine is planted: but when it is grown we know it not, because it has taken up the whole.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence did it begin?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “At Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whither has it come?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: To “all nations.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A few remain: it shall possess all.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the mean time, while it is taking possession of all, it has seemed good to the Husbandman to cut off some unprofitable branches, and they have made heresies and schisms.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let not the branches that are cut off induce you to be cut off: rather exhort ye them that are cut off that they be grafted in again.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is manifest that Christ hath suffered, is risen again, and is ascended into heaven: made manifest also is the Church, because there is “preached in His name repentance and remission of sins throughout all nations.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence did it begin?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Beginning at Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The man hears this; foolish and vain, and (how, shall I express it?) worse than blind! so great a mountain, and he does not see it; a candle set upon a candlestick, and he shuts his eyes against it!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When we say to them, If ye be Catholic Christians, communicate with that Church from which the Gospel is spread abroad over the whole earth: communicate with that Jerusalem: when this we say to them, they make answer to us, we do not communicate with that city where our King was slain, where our Lord was slain: as though they hate the city where our Lord was slain.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The Jews slew Him whom they found on earth, these scorn Him that sitteth in heaven!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Which are the worse; those who despised Him because they thought Him man, or those who scorn the sacraments of Him whom now they confess to be God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But they hate, forsooth, the city in which their Lord was slain!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Pious men, and merciful! they much grieve that Christ was slain, and in men they slay Christ!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But He loved that city, and pitied it: from it He bade the preaching of Him begin, “beginning at Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He made there the beginning of the preaching of His name: and thou shrinkest back with horror from having communion with that city!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: No marvel that being cut off thou hatest the root.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What said He to His disciples?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Sit ye still in the city, because I send my promise upon you.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Behold what the city is that they hate!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Haply they would love it, if Christ’s murderers dwelt in it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For it is manifest that all Christ’s murderers, i.e., the Jews, are expelled from that city.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That which had in it them that were fierce against Christ, hath now them that adore Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore do these men hate it, because Christians are in it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There was it His will that His disciples should tarry, and there that He should send to them the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Where had the Church its commencement, but where the Holy Ghost came from heaven, and filled the hundred and twenty sitting in one place?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That number twelve was made tenfold.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: They sat, an hundred and twenty persons, and the Holy Ghost came, “and filled the whole place, and there came a sound, as it were the rushing of a mighty wind, and there were cloven tongues like as of fire.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye have heard the Acts of the Apostles: this was the lesson read today: “They began to speak with tongues as the Spirit gave them utterance.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And all who were on the spot, Jews who were come from divers nations, recognised each his own tongue, and marvelled that those unlearned and ignorant men had on the sudden learned not one or two tongues, but the tongues of all nations whatsoever.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There, then, where all tongues sounded, there was it betokened that all tongues should believe.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But these men, who much love Christ, and therefore refuse to communicate with the city which killed Christ, so honor Christ as to affirm that He is left to two tongues, the Latin and the Punic, i.e. African.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Christ possess only two tongues!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For there are but these two tongues on the side of Donatus, more they have not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let us awake, my brethren, let us rather see the gift of the Spirit of God, and let us believe the things spoken before concerning Him, and let us see fulfilled the things spoken before in the Psalm: “There are neither speeches nor discourses, but their voices are heard among them.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And lest haply the case be so that the tongues themselves came to one place, and not rather that the gift of Christ came to all tongues, hear what follows: “Into all the earth is their sound gone out, and unto the ends of the world their words.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wherefore this?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because “in the sun hath He set His tabernacle,” i.e., in the open light.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: His tabernacle, His flesh: His tabernacle, His Church: “in the sun” it is set; not in the night, but in the day.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But why do those not acknowledge it?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Return to the lesson at the place where it ended yesterday, and see why they do not acknowledge it: “He that hateth his brother, walketh in darkness, and knoweth not whither he goeth, because the darkness hath blinded his eyes.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For us then, let us see what follows, and not be in darkness.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How shall we not be in darkness?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If we love the brethren.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How is it proved that we love the brotherhood?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By this, that we do not rend unity, that we hold fast charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I write unto you, little children, because your sins are forgiven you through His name.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore, “little children,” because in forgiveness of sins ye have your birth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But through whose name are sins forgiven?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Through Augustin’s?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: No, therefore neither through the name of Donatus.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Be it thy concern to see who is Augustin, or who Donatus: no, not through the name of Paul, not through the name of Peter.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For to them that divided unto themselves the Church, and out of unity essayed to make parties, the mother charity in the apostle travailing in birth with her little ones, exposeth her own bowels, with words doth as it were rend her breasts, bewaileth her children whom she seeth borne out dead, recalleth unto the one Name them that would needs make them many names, repelleth them from the love of her that Christ may be loved, and saith, “Was Paul crucified for you?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What saith he?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I would not that ye be mine, that so ye may be with me: be ye with me; all we are His who died for us, who was crucified for us”: whence here also it is said, “Your sins are forgiven you through His name,” not through the name of any man.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I write unto you, fathers.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why first sons?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Because your sins are forgiven you through His name,” and ye are regenerated into a new life, therefore sons.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why fathers?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Because ye have known Him that is from the beginning:” for the beginning hath relation unto fatherhood.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Christ new in flesh, but ancient in Godhead.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How ancient think we? how many years old?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Think we, of greater age than His mother?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Assuredly of greater age than His mother, for “all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If all things, then did the Ancient make the very mother of whom the New should be born.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Was He, think we, before His mother only?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Yea, and before His mother’s ancestors is His antiquity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The ancestor of His mother was Abraham; and the Lord saith, “Before Abraham I am.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Before Abraham, say we?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The heaven and earth, ere man was, were made.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Before these was the Lord, nay rather also is.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For right well He saith, not, Before Abraham I was, but, “Before Abraham I Am.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For that of which one says, “was,” is not; and that of which one says, “will be,” is not yet: He knoweth not other than to be.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: As God, He knoweth “to be:” “was,” and “will be,” He knoweth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It is one day there, but a day that is for ever and ever.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That day yesterday and tomorrow do not set in the midst between them: for when the ‘yesterday’ is ended, the ‘to-day’ begins, to be finished by the coming ‘tomorrow.’

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That one day there is a day without darkness, without night, without spaces, without measure, without hours.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Call it what thou wilt: if thou wilt, it is a day; if thou wilt, a year; if thou wilt, years.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For it is said of this same, “And thy years shall not fail.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But when is it called a day?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When it is said to the Lord, “To-day have I begotten Thee.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: From the eternal Father begotten, from eternity begotten, in eternity begotten: with no beginning, no bound, no space of breadth; because He is what is, because Himself is “He that Is.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This His name He told to Moses: “Thou shalt say unto them, He that Is hath sent me unto you.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why speak then of “before Abraham”? why, before Noe? why, before Adam?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear the Scripture: “Before the day-star have I begotten Thee.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In fine, before heaven and earth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By this know ye the “fathers:” for they become fathers by acknowledging “That which is from the beginning.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I write unto you, young men.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There are sons, are fathers, are young men: sons, because begotten; fathers, because they acknowledge the Beginning; why young men?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Because ye have overcome the wicked one.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the sons, birth: in the fathers, antiquity: in the young men, strength.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If the wicked one is “overcome” by the young men, he fights with us.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Fights, but not conquers.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because we are strong, or because He is strong in us who in the hands of the persecutors was found weak?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He hath made us strong, who resisted not His persecutors.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “For He was crucified of weakness, but He liveth by the power of God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I write unto you, children.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whence children?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Because ye have known the Father.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I write unto you fathers:” he enforceth this, and repeateth, “Because ye have known Him that is from the beginning.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Remember that ye are fathers: if ye forget “Him that is from the beginning,” ye have lost your fatherhood.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I write unto you, young men.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Again and again consider that ye are young men: fight, that ye may overcome: overcome, that ye may be crowned: be lowly, that ye fall not in the fight.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “I write unto you, young men, because ye are strong, and the word of God abideth in you, and ye have overcome the wicked one.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: All these things, my brethren,—“because we have known That which is from the beginning, because we are strong, because we have known the Father,”—do all these, while they in a manner commend knowledge, not commend charity?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If we have known, let us love: for knowledge without charity saveth not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Knowledge puffeth up, charity edifieth.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If ye have a mind to confess and not love, ye begin to be like the demons.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The demons confessed the Son of God, and said, “What have we to do with Thee?” and were repulsed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Confess and embrace.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For those feared for their iniquities; love ye Him that forgiveth your iniquities.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But how can we love God, if we love the world?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He prepareth us therefore to be inhabited by charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There are two loves: of the world, and of God: if the love of the world inhabit, there is no way for the love of God to enter in: let the love of the world make way, and the love of God inhabit; let the better have place.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou lovedst the world: love not the world: when thou hast emptied thine heart of earthly love, thou shalt drink in love Divine: and thenceforth beginneth charity to inhabit thee, from which can nothing of evil proceed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear ye therefore his words, how he goes to work in the manner of one that makes a clearance.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He comes upon the hearts of men as a field that he would occupy: but in what state does he find it?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If he finds a wood, he roots it up; if he finds the field cleared, he plants it.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He would plant a tree there, charity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And what is the wood he would root up?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Love of the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hear him, the rooter up of the wood!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Love not the world,” (for this comes next,) “neither the things that are in the world; if any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Ye have heard that “if any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let not any say in his heart that this is false, brethren: God saith it; by the Apostle the Holy Ghost hath spoken; nothing more true: “If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou have the Father’s love, that thou mayest be joint-heir with the Son?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Love not the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Shut out the evil love of the world, that thou mayest be filled with the love of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou art a vessel; but as yet thou art full.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Pour out what thou hast, that thou mayest receive what thou hast not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Certainly, our brethren are now born again of water and of the Spirit: we also some years ago were born again of water and of the Spirit.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Good is it for us that we love not the world, lest the sacraments remain in us unto damnation, not as means of strengthening unto salvation.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: That which strengthens unto salvation is, to have the root of charity, to have the “power of godliness,” not “the form” only.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Good is the form, holy the form: but what avails the form, if it hold not the root?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The branch that is cut off, is it not cast into the fire?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Have the form, but in the root.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But in what way are ye rooted so that ye be not rooted up?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By holding charity, as saith the Apostle Paul, “rooted and grounded in charity.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How shall charity be rooted there, amid the overgrown wilderness of the love of the world?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Make clear riddance of the woods.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: A mighty seed ye are about to put in: let there not be that in the field which shall choke the seed.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These are the uprooting words which he hath said: “Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “For all that is in the world, is the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life,” three things he hath said, which are not of the Father, but are of the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever, even as He abideth for ever.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why am I not to love what God made?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What wilt thou?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Whether wilt thou love the things of time, and pass away with time; or not love the world, and live to eternity with God?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The river of temporal things hurries one along: but like a tree sprung up beside the river is our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He assumed flesh, died, rose again, ascended into heaven.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: It was His will to plant Himself, in a manner, beside the river of the things of time.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Art thou rushing down the stream to the headlong deep?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hold fast the tree.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is love of the world whirling thee on?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hold fast Christ.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For thee He became temporal, that thou mightest become eternal; because He also in such sort became temporal, that He remained still eternal.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Something was added to Him from time, not anything went from His eternity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But thou wast born temporal, and by sin wast made temporal: thou wast made temporal by sin, He was made temporal by mercy in remitting sins.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: How great the difference, when two are in a prison, between the criminal and him that visits him!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For upon a time a person comes to his friend and enters in to visit him, and both seem to be in prison; but they differ by a wide distinction.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The one, his cause presses down: the other, humanity has brought thither.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: So in this our mortal state, we were held fast by our guiltiness, He in mercy came down: He entered in unto the captive, a Redeemer not an oppressor.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The Lord for us shed His blood, redeemed us, changed our hope.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: As yet we bear the mortality of the flesh, and take the future immortality upon trust: and on the sea we are tossed by the waves, but we have the anchor of hope already fixed upon the land.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But let us “not love the world, neither the things that are in the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For the things that are in the world, are the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These three are they: lest haply any man say, “The things that are in the world, God made: i.e. heaven and earth, the sea: the sun, the moon, the stars, all the garniture of the heavens.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What is the garniture of the sea? all creeping things.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What of the earth? animals, trees, flying creatures.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These are ‘in the world,’ God made them.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Why then am I not to love what God hath made?”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Let the Spirit of God be in thee, that thou mayest see that all these things are good: but woe to thee if thou love the things made, and forsake the Maker of them!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Fair are they to thee: but how much fairer He that formed them!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark well, beloved.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For by similitudes ye may be instructed: lest Satan steal upon you, saying what he is wont to say, Take your enjoyment in the creature of God; wherefore made He those things but for your enjoyment?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And men drink themselves drunken, and perish, and forget their own Creator: while not temperately but lustfully they use the things created, the Creator is despised.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Of such saith the apostle: “They worshipped and served the creature rather than the Creator, Who is blessed for ever.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: God doth not forbid thee to love these things, howbeit, not to set thine affections upon them for blessedness, but to approve and praise them to this end, that thou mayest love thy Creator.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In the same manner, my brethren, as if a bridegroom should make a ring for his bride, and she having received the ring, should love it more than she loves the bridegroom who made the ring for her: would not her soul be found guilty of adultery in the very gift of the bridegroom, albeit she did but love what the bridegroom gave her?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By all means let her love what the bridegroom gave: yet should she say, “This ring is enough for me, I do not wish to see his face now:” what sort of woman would she be?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Who would not detest such folly? who not pronounce her guilty of an adulterous mind?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou lovest gold in place of the man, lovest a ring in place of the bridegroom: if this be in thee, that thou lovest a ring in place of thy bridegroom, and hast no wish to see thy bridegroom; that he has given thee an earnest, serves not to pledge thee to him, but to turn away thy heart from him!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For this the bridegroom gives earnest, that in his earnest he may himself be loved.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Well then, God gave thee all these things: love Him that made them.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: There is more that He would fain give thee, that is, His very Self that made these things.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But if thou love these—what though God made them—and neglect the Creator and love the world; shall not thy love be accounted adulterous?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For “the world” is the appellation given not only to this fabric which God made heaven and earth, the sea, things visible and invisible: but the inhabitants of the world are called the world, just as we call a “house” both the walls and them that inhabit therein.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: And sometimes we praise a house, and find fault with the inhabitants.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For we say, A good house; because it is marbled and beautifully ceiled: and in another sense we say, A good house: no man there suffers wrong, no acts of plunder, no acts of oppression, are done there.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now we praise not the building, but those who dwell within the building: yet we call it “house,” both this and that.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For all lovers of the world, because by love they inhabit the world, just as those inhabit heaven, whose heart is on high while in the flesh they walk on earth: I say then, all lovers of the world are called the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The same have only these three things, “lust of the flesh, lust of the eyes, vain glory of life.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For they lust to eat, drink, cohabit: to use these pleasures.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Not surely, that there is no allowed measure in these things, or that when it is said, Love not these things, it means that ye are not to eat, or not to drink, or not to beget children?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This is not the thing said.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Only, let there be measure, because of the Creator, that these things may not bind you by your loving of them: lest ye love that for enjoyment, which ye ought to have for use.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But ye are not put to the proof except when two things are propounded to you, this or that: Will thou righteousness or gains?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I have not wherewithal to live, have not wherewithal to eat, have not wherewithal to drink.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But what if thou canst not have these but by iniquity?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it not better to love that which thou losest not, than to lose thyself by iniquity?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Thou seest the gain of gold, the loss of faith thou seest not.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This then, saith he to us, is “the lust of the flesh,” i.e. the lusting after those things which pertain to the flesh, such as food, and carnal cohabitation, and all other such like.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And the lust of the eyes:” by “the lust of the eyes,” he means all curiosity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Now how wide is the scope of curiosity!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: This it is that works in spectacles, in theatres, in sacraments of the devil, in magical arts, in dealings with darkness: none other than curiosity.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Sometimes it tempts even the servants of God, so that they wish as it were to work a miracle, to tempt God whether He will hear their prayers in working of miracles; it is curiosity: this is “lust of the eyes;” it “is not of the Father.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If God hath given the power, do the miracle, for He hath put it in thy way to do it: for think not that those who have not done miracles shall not pertain to the kingdom of God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When the apostles were rejoicing that the demons were subject to them, what said the Lord to them?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “Rejoice not in this, but rejoice because your names are written in heaven.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: In that would He have the apostles to rejoice, wherein thou also rejoicest.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Woe to thee truly if thy name be not written in heaven!

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Is it woe to thee if thou raise not the dead? is it woe to thee if thou walk not on the sea? is it woe to thee if thou cast not out demons?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If thou hast received power to do them, use it humbly, not proudly.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For even of certain false prophets the Lord hath said that “they shall do signs and prodigies.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Therefore let there be no “ambition of the world:” Ambitio sæculi, is Pride.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: The man wishes to make much of himself in his honors: he thinks himself great, whether because of riches, or because of some power.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: These three there are, and thou canst find nothing whereby human cupidity can be tempted, but either by the lust of the flesh, or the lust of the eyes, or the pride of life.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By these three was the Lord tempted of the devil.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By the lust of the flesh He was tempted when it was said to Him, “If thou be the Son of God, speak to these stones that they become bread,” when He hungered after His fast.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But in what way repelled He the tempter, and taught his soldier how to fight?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Mark what He said to him: “Not by bread alone doth man live, but by every word of God.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He was tempted also by the lust of the eyes concerning a miracle, when he said to Him, “Cast thyself down: for it is written, He shall give his angels charge concerning thee: and in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He resisted the tempter, for to do the miracle, would only have been to seem either to have yielded, or to have done it from curiosity; for He wrought when He would, as God, howbeit as healing the weak.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For if He had done it then, He might have been thought to wish only to do a miracle.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: But lest men should think this, mark what He answered; and when the like temptation shall happen to thee, say thou also the same: “Get thee behind me, Satan; for it is written, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God:” that is, if I do this I shall tempt God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: He said what He would have thee to say.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When the enemy suggests to thee, “What sort of man, what sort of Christian, art thou?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: As yet hast thou done one miracle, or by thy prayers have the dead been raised, or hast thou healed the fevered? if thou wert truly of any moment, thou wouldest do some miracle:” answer and say: “It is written, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God:” therefore I will not tempt God, as if I should belong to God if I do a miracle, and not belong if I do none: and what becomes then of His words, “Rejoice, because your names are written in heaven”?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By “pride of life” how was the Lord tempted?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: When he carried Him up to an high place, and said to Him, “All these will I give thee, if thou wilt fall down and worship me.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: By the loftiness of an earthly kingdom he wished to tempt the King of all worlds: but the Lord who made heaven and earth trod the devil under foot.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: What great matter for the devil to be conquered by the Lord?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Then what did He in the answer He made to the devil but teach thee the answer He would have thee to make?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “It is, written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and Him only shalt thou serve.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Holding these things fast, ye shall not have the concupiscence of the world: by not having concupiscence of the world, neither shall the lust of the flesh, nor the lust of the eyes, nor the pride of life, subjugate you: and ye shall make place for Charity when she cometh, that ye may love God.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Because if love of the world be there, love of God will not be there.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Hold fast rather the love of God, that as God is for ever and ever, so ye also may remain for ever and ever: because such is each one as is his love.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Lovest thou earth, thou shalt be earth.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: Lovest thou God, what shall I say? thou shalt be a god?

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: I darenot say it of myself, let us hear the Scriptures: “I have said, Ye are gods, and all of you sons of the Most High.”

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If then ye would be gods and sons of the Most High, “Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: For all the things that are in the world, is the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, which is not of the Father, but is of the world:” i.e. of men, lovers of the world.

verses.book: Ten Homilies on the First Epistle of John.
verses.text: “And the world passeth away, and the lusts thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever, even as God also abideth for ever.”
